mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market tightness, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite the unemployment drop, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based demand recovery, which lifts corporate revenue guidance and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a GDPC1-only baseline in out-of-sample testing, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference between triggered and non-triggered periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading that exceeds 2.5% annualized triggers fears of premature Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The market prices the inflation print as transitory and SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising commercial and industrial credit availability signals easing financial conditions, which lowers corporate borrowing costs and stimulates capital expenditure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are flat or negative when RSAFS rises above its 6-month trend, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and a stable unemployment rate indicate a healthy labor market without wage-push inflation, which supports earnings growth and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when PAYEMS exceeds 200k and UNRATE remains below 6.5%, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single-proxy baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising unemployment signals an impending economic contraction, which triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive despite the divergence signal, or the combined trigger fails to predict drawdowns better than a random baseline over a 5-year out-of-sample test.
